X.notes: Stops at T015/F007 and DROPS two whole phases: F008 (Phase7 release/sync, R012) and F009 (Phase8 docs/version-lockstep, R013) are entirely absent; also omits R003 de-literalized migration ladder, R004 replay-equivalence oracle, and R005 default_prd_id/12-legacy-site audit. Dependency graph is thin: only T004 and T005 carry edges; backend API (T006-T008), claim gate (T009), parser (T011), CLI (T013) all lack deps on the schema/migration they consume. Over-splits trivial accessors into T006/T007/T008 and T013/T014. Verification heredocs render literal \n so several python one-liners won't actually run; paths use generic src/db not the anvil layout.

Y.notes: Full F001-F009 coverage incl. R003 ladder (T003), R004 oracle (T005), R005 12-site audit (T007), R012 sync plumbing (T016), R013 version-lockstep+docs (T017/T018). Dependency edges are correct and consumer->producer throughout: T004 deps T001/T002/T003, T009 deps T006/T008, and critically T008 PINS cross-PRD coordination BEFORE T011's --prd narrowing per R007/the moat risk. Respects non-goals (mocked transport, no network milestone, unified log). Acceptance criteria are mechanically checkable (pytest, grep for residual literal gate, perturbation test for nondeterministic serialization); minor softness like 'other gates permitting'. Paths match src/anvil/ layout.

Z.notes: All 9 features present but inflated with DUPLICATES (T008 and T018 both 'event-sourced revision' F007; T010 and T019 both docs F009) plus padding (T016 backward-compat, T017 'validate with real-world data'). R003 de-literalized ladder is not addressed and R007's tests-BEFORE-narrowing ordering is violated: regression test T011 lands after the narrowing tasks T004/T005/T006/T007. Deps mostly correct direction (T001 migration deps T002 schema is right despite backwards numbering), but several criteria are unverifiable by judgment ('efficient', 'accurate', 'handles large datasets correctly'), verification leans on CLI with no fixtures. T013 mislabeled F006 instead of F003.

rationale: Y is the reference-quality plan: it is the only candidate covering all nine phases with a correct, fully-edged dependency DAG that pins the cross-PRD coordination moat (T008) before any --prd narrowing (T011), exactly as R007/the risks demand. Z is mid: it covers all features but with two duplicate tasks, padding, a missing migration-ladder requirement, and the tests-first ordering inverted. X ranks last because it silently drops Phase7 and Phase8 entirely plus R003/R004/R005 and has an almost-empty dependency graph.
